title: selago retropilosa
intro: Selago retropilosa, also known as the Cape pegtop, is a flowering plant native to the Western Cape province of South Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, treating fever, and aiding in digestion. In traditional herbal preparations, Selago retropilosa is often used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use should be guided by a healthcare professional due to the potential side effects.
